items.mistake: Leaving `justMyCode = true` when investigating a library bug.
items.fix: Set `justMyCode = false` when you need to step into numpy/openai/etc.
items.why: Default skips library code; you'll skip past the very frames that matter.

items.fix: Wrap with an env-var guard (`if os.environ.get('DEBUG_WAIT') == '1':`).
items.why: `wait_for_client()` blocks forever in a non-interactive environment — the process appears hung.

items.mistake: Forgetting `pathMappings` when attaching to a containerized process.
items.fix: Map local repo to the container path: `{ "localRoot": "${workspaceFolder}", "remoteRoot": "/app" }`.
items.why: Without the mapping, gutter breakpoints in the IDE don't match the container's path; they never trigger.
items.example: Symptom: 'breakpoints work locally but never trigger when attached'. Cause: missing pathMappings.

items.fix: Commit both `.vscode/launch.json` and a `pytest --pdb` / `python -m pdb` README equivalent.
items.why: Teammates on other editors are otherwise stuck.
items.example: README: 'VS Code: see launch.json. PyCharm: see Run Configurations. CLI: `pytest --pdb -x`.'

items.mistake: Relying on visual breakpoints for an entire async pipeline without `justMyCode = false`.
items.fix: Disable `justMyCode` AND enable async-aware stepping in the IDE's debug settings.
items.why: Without those, you step over `await`s into nothing and miss the actual task interactions.
items.example: VS Code + Python: 'Async stepping' and 'Show return value' in settings.
